sourceMovie.title: Doctor Sleep
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Doctor Sleep (2019) stuck because it commits to its horror premise without apologizing—viewers remember set pieces and momentum as much as plot. Mike Flanagan's control of tone keeps the ride coherent. The horror and fantasy blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Beetlejuice Beetlejuice
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Doctor Sleep clicked for you, Beetlejuice Beetlejuice (2024) is a strong next stop: both trade in horror and fantasy, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: Howl's Moving Castle
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Doctor Sleep clicked for you, Howl's Moving Castle (2004) is a strong next stop: both trade in fantasy, with earned warmth without mush. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: Beetlejuice
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Doctor Sleep clicked for you, Beetlejuice (1988) is a strong next stop: both trade in fantasy, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: Insidious
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Doctor Sleep clicked for you, Insidious (2011) is a strong next stop: both trade in horror, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Doctor Sleep?
faq.answer: Try Beetlejuice Beetlejuice, Howl's Moving Castle, Beetlejuice—each shares DNA with Doctor Sleep while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Doctor Sleep?
faq.answer: Doctor Sleep (2019) is best described as Horror, Fantasy. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Doctor Sleep part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "The Shining Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
